assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSMENT 1:
Post-crisis calibration bias is a real concern addressed by the documented stress supplement. Two-tier PD approach (base-case vs. stress) is standard credit model governance. Loan officer scores are industry-standard with established audit procedures. Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSMENT 2:
The 2010–2020 training window captures a benign credit environment. The team addresses this with a stress supplement applying 2007–2009 default rate overlays for capital purposes. This is the correct methodological response. Loan officer qualitative scores are permissible and auditable. Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSMENT 3:
Both validator concerns are addressed in the documentation. The stress supplement is explicitly described as the response to benign-cycle calibration. Loan officer scores are standard in mid-market commercial lending. Neither concern warrants model rejection. Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins. Stress supplement addresses the post-crisis calibration concern. Loan officer score concern is a governance monitoring matter. No contested empirical claim requiring test specification.
